title: Term Sheet Logic Operator Lesson
objective: Understand the operator fundamentals behind term sheet logic in capital / fundraising.
beginnerExplanation: Term Sheet Logic becomes easier when you break it into signal, decisions, owners, and follow-through. This lesson gives you a practical structure instead of abstract advice.
deeperExplanation: At higher levels, term sheet logic is about tradeoffs. You are deciding where to focus, what to ignore, and how to keep the work legible enough for other people to trust.
keyTakeaways: Term Sheet Logic improves when the operating goal is explicit.
You need evidence, not just motion, to judge whether term sheet logic is working.
Term Sheet Logic should leave behind reusable assets like notes, decisions, scorecards, or systems.
example: A founder or operator uses term sheet logic to clarify what changed, what matters next, and what must happen before the next review.
commonMistakes: Treating term sheet logic like isolated activity instead of part of a larger system.
Skipping the review loop and assuming the first answer is good enough.
Letting ownership stay vague after the decision is made.
exercise: Write a one-page note explaining how you would improve term sheet logic over the next two weeks inside a real business.
